dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 7572 IH: No Vigilante Checkpoints and Civil Rights Protection Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Crenshaw introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on the Judiciary

form.official-title: To protect the civil rights of individuals against unlawful vigilante checkpoints and identity demands, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the No Vigilante Checkpoints and Civil Rights Protection Act of 2026.

paragraph.text: The Fourth Amendment protects the right of individuals to be free from unreasonable searches and seizures, including unlawful vehicle stops and demands for identification.

In recent years, private individuals and organizations have engaged in conduct resembling law enforcement activities, including vehicle stops, identity checks, and license plate surveillance, without lawful authority.
Such conduct undermines public safety, interferes with Federal operations, and deprives individuals of rights secured by the Constitution and laws of the United States.
Congress has authority under article I, section 8, and section 5 of the Fourteenth Amendment to protect civil rights and ensure the unobstructed execution of Federal law.

section.header: Prohibition on false assumption of government authority in immigration enforcement

subsection.text: Chapter 33 of title 18, United States Code, is amended by inserting after section 709 the following:

section.header: False assumption of government authority in law enforcement

paragraph.text: stops, detains, or attempts to stop or detain any individual or vehicle on a public roadway;
demands or requests identification;
demands or requests to search property or persons; or
uses or operates any device or system to collect, access, or analyze license plate or vehicle identification data.

subsection.continuation-text: For the purpose of enforcing, monitoring, obstructing, or influencing Federal law enforcement operations, shall be guilty of an offense under this section.

subsection.text: A violation of this section shall be punishable by—

paragraph.text: imprisonment for not more than 5 years;
a fine under this title; or
both.
involves the use or display of a firearm or other dangerous weapon;
results in bodily injury; or
interferes with or targets a Federal officer or Federal operation;

subsection.continuation-text: the offender shall be imprisoned for not more than 10 years.

subsection.text: Nothing in this section shall be construed to prohibit lawful protest, speech, or assembly that does not involve conduct described in subsection (a).

section.header: Civil action for unlawful checkpoints and identity demands

subsection.text: Any person aggrieved by conduct prohibited under section 709A of title 18, United States Code, may bring a civil action in an appropriate United States district court against—

paragraph.text: any individual who engaged in such conduct;
any organization that directed, financed, coordinated, or materially supported such conduct; or
any governmental entity that knowingly permitted, authorized, or failed to take reasonable steps to prevent such conduct on public property under its control.

subsection.text: In a civil action under this section, a court may award—

paragraph.text: statutory damages of not less than $10,000 per violation, without regard to proof of actual damages;
compensatory damages;
punitive damages, where appropriate;
injunctive or declaratory relief; and
reasonable attorneys’ fees and costs.

subsection.text: Qualified immunity or any similar defense shall not be available to any defendant in an action brought under this section.
An action under this section may be brought not later than 5 years after the date on which the violation occurred.

section.text: If any provision of this Act, or the application thereof to any person or circumstance, is held invalid, the remainder of the Act and the application of such provision to other persons or circumstances shall not be affected.
This Act shall take effect on the date of its enactment.
